title: Maya R. — Follow-up visit (8 weeks)
description: Follow-up state: PedMIDAS improving after lifestyle plan + riboflavin, ibuprofen use reduced, referral watch item retained.

history.label: First headache visit (Bridge summary)
history.text: Migraine-compatible recurrent headaches, ~8 days/month, PedMIDAS 32, red-flag screen negative. Plan: lifestyle changes, headache diary, limit ibuprofen <3 days/week, riboflavin discussed and started after clinician review.

history.label: Nurse follow-up call
history.text: Family reports diary being kept most days. Headaches somewhat less frequent. No new symptoms. Riboflavin tolerated well.

red_flag_catalog.label: Headache waking from sleep / worst on waking

pedmidas_questions.question: Full school days missed in last 3 months
Partial school days missed in last 3 months
School days functioning at less than half
Days unable to do home activities/chores
Days participated in activities at less than half

evidence.quote: It's down to maybe one a week now, and she's only left school early once since summer.
She takes the ibuprofen maybe once a week now, and the riboflavin every morning.
Nothing new — no night headaches, no vomiting, nothing scary.
Diary being kept most days. Headaches somewhat less frequent. Riboflavin tolerated well.

school_impact.value: One early dismissal since last visit

changes_since_last_visit: Headache frequency down from ~8 to ~4 days/month [evb-1, evb-2]
Ibuprofen use reduced from ~3x/week to ~1x/week — overuse concern resolved [evb-3]

summary: 8-week follow-up: headache frequency down ~8 → ~4 days/month on lifestyle plan + riboflavin. [evb-1, evb-2]
PedMIDAS improved 32 → 11; school and activity participation recovering. [evb-1, evb-2, evb-5]
Acute ibuprofen use reduced to ~1x/week; overuse concern resolved. [evb-3]
Red-flag screen remains negative at follow-up. [evb-4]
questions_to_ask: Any aura or new symptom types since last visit?
suggested_pathway: Continue current lifestyle plan and riboflavin (clinician review required)
Continue diary; reassess PedMIDAS at next follow-up
referral_considerations: Neurology referral not indicated while improving (clinician to confirm)
Escalate if trend reverses, disability rises, or red flags appear
patient_instructions: Keep going — the plan is working
Continue diary and riboflavin as reviewed with your clinician
Return sooner for night waking headaches, morning vomiting, or sudden changes
care_plan.disclaimer: Draft for clinician review. Final clinical decisions remain with the clinician.

canned_qa.answer: Since the July 18 visit: headache frequency dropped from ~8 to ~4 days/month, PedMIDAS improved from 32 to 11, ibuprofen use fell from ~3x/week to ~1x/week, and riboflavin has been tolerated well. The red-flag screen remains negative.

No neurology referral is suggested while the trend is improving — this remains a clinician decision. The draft plan retains an escalation trigger: refer if the trend reverses, disability rises, or any red flag appears.
